testCases.query: Set up a production Celery app with Redis broker
expectedBehavior: Uses JSON serializer and never allows pickle deserialization
Sets task_acks_late=True to prevent task loss
Configures both task_time_limit and task_soft_time_limit for graceful termination
Sets worker_prefetch_multiplier=1 for fair task distribution across workers

testCases.query: Route tasks to priority queues based on user tier
expectedBehavior: Defines critical/high/default/low priority queues using kombu Queue declarations
Configures broker_transport_options with priority_steps for queue priority support
Implements TaskRouter class for dynamic routing
Assigns dedicated workers per queue with tuned concurrency

testCases.query: Build an ETL pipeline with parallel processing and aggregation
expectedBehavior: Uses chain for sequential extract-transform-load steps
Uses chord for parallel chunk processing with callback
Implements link_error for workflow-level error handling
Uses si() for immutable signatures in appropriate steps

testCases.query: Implement a task with retry and idempotency protection
expectedBehavior: Uses autoretry_for with retry_backoff=True and retry_jitter=True
Implements idempotency key check in Redis before processing
Sets max_retries with retry_backoff_max ceiling to bound delay growth
Moves permanently failed tasks to dead letter queue

testCases.query: Configure periodic tasks with overlap prevention
expectedBehavior: Uses celery.schedules.crontab for flexible time-based periodic scheduling
Implements Redis-based schedule lock to prevent overlap
Configures beat_schedule with explicit UTC timezone for consistency
Separates beat scheduler process from worker processes in production

testCases.query: Add monitoring and health checks to Celery workers
expectedBehavior: Connects to celery signals for task_prerun and task_failure
Implements health check verifying broker and active workers
Monitors queue depths for autoscaling decisions
Uses Flower with persistent storage for dashboard

testCases.query: Track task progress with custom states
expectedBehavior: Tracks job status updates via update_state with progress metadata
Uses update_state() for real-time progress reporting to clients
Queries progress via AsyncResult with state checks
Stores large results in S3/DB, not Redis

testCases.query: Set up async background tasks with ARQ and FastAPI
expectedBehavior: Creates ARQ worker with startup/shutdown lifecycle hooks
Enqueues jobs from FastAPI routes with enqueue_job()
Implements job status endpoint with Job.status()
Configures max_jobs and job_timeout in WorkerSettings

testCases.query: Set up a Temporal activity for payment processing with proper heartbeat and retry configuration
expectedBehavior: Implements activity heartbeat for long-running operations to report progress to Temporal
Classifies errors into retryable and non-retryable using ApplicationError with non_retryable flag
Configures start_to_close_timeout and heartbeat_timeout for activity timeout management
Sets up proper worker configuration with appropriate task queue and activity registration

testCases.query: Define a Temporal workflow for order processing that handles signals and maintains determinism
expectedBehavior: Keeps workflow code deterministic by avoiding random, datetime.now, and direct IO operations
Uses workflow.wait_condition for signal handling instead of direct state manipulation
Calls all IO operations through activities rather than executing them directly in workflow code
Implements saga pattern with compensating activities for multi-step transaction rollback
